reflection:
your heart was at 85 when you woke at 5:50, and you wrote that the second you became aware of yourself it started pounding. that tracks with the whole week. anxiety has climbed 2, 3, 5, 7 across the last four mornings, and the waking heart-race went 4 to 8 right alongside it. this isn't the earplugs. it's the speech on the 2nd. your body is rehearsing fear two days early.

the dream about Olle leaving you out, the worry that people only stick around because they need you, you already took that one apart yourself. you ran the thought record, dropped it from an 8 to a 6, and landed on the real version. they're your employees, there's room for improvement, the hair isn't you. that is the work doing exactly what it's built to do. when the morning brain hands you the shit sandwich, you don't have to eat it whole anymore.

you also walked 12km, got a good massage, ate with Murat, and got the speech to a place where you said you're finally getting comfortable. hold onto that last line. you also wrote that what you're avoiding is practising it. so that's the one thing today. do one full run, out loud, start to finish. give your nervous system a track to follow, so tomorrow morning it has something rehearsed to grab instead of fear.

you are enough. you told yourself that this morning. believe the version of you that wrote it.
opener: your 5am heart-race is the speech, not the earplugs. and you know it.
focus_for_today: do one full out-loud run of the speech, start to finish, to settle tomorrow's nerves
